name: Diego sends a v2 quote after the customer declined the original on price
description: Customer declined v1 with feedback ('a bit high — could you do $400?'). The /assistant surfaces the rejection in the same thread, suggests a re-quote, and lets Diego adjust line items. The new quote (v2) is linked to v1 via a 'revisionOf' reference so the timeline reads as one negotiation, not two unrelated quotes.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Got a polite no on the fence quote; wants to come down $30 without losing margin.

preconditions: Quote v1 exists with status='lost' and lostReason='price'
Conversation thread for this quote is still active

steps.action: Customer rejection lands; thread receives a phase_event divider 'Customer declined — price'
steps.expect: Assistant nudges: 'Want to send a revised version?'

steps.action: On /assistant: 'Yes, drop labor by $30; keep materials'
steps.expect: LLM emits create_quote action with v1.lineItems but labor reduced by 3000 cents; payload includes revisionOf=<v1 id>
QuoteStore.create writes v2 with revisionOf reference; email sent; /quotes shows v2 in 'Out for response' with a small 'v2 · revision of v1' chip
Page header includes 'Revised quote — replaces an earlier estimate'; v1 link is read-only

evaluationCriteria.check: v2 chip in /quotes makes the relationship visible at a glance; not a separate orphaned card
Customer reads v2 as 'continued conversation', not 'second sales push'
QuoteCard rendering reads revisionOf and links back to v1 in the timeline; analytics treat them as one funnel, not two
Revision chip has descriptive aria-label ('Revision of quote V1, sent April 28')
